# Power BI Report Blueprint — Technology Risk Analytics

**Semantic model:** `examples/powerbi/export/` (generated by `powerbi-export`)  
**Measures:** [dax/measures.md](dax/measures.md)  
**RLS:** [rls_design.md](rls_design.md)

This blueprint maps the portfolio star schema to four interview-ready report pages aligned with PL-300 storytelling.

---

## Page 1: Executive Overview

**Audience:** Risk Committee, CIO, Head of Technology Risk

| Visual | Fields / measure |
|--------|------------------|
| Card | Total Incidents |
| Card | High Risk Incidents |
| Card | Unresolved Incidents |
| Card | Control Failure Rate |
| Donut | `risk_level` distribution |
| Bar | Incident count by `dim_classification[classification]` |

**Slicers:** Year, Quarter, `risk_level`, `proof_tier_key`

**Narrative:** Summarize endpoint reliability exposure — not malware prevalence. High preview-only counts indicate policy gates working.

**Footer:** Classification is not accusation. Management information — not a formal audit opinion.

---

## Page 2: Risk Trend

**Audience:** Technology Risk, IT Operations leadership

| Visual | Fields / measure |
|--------|------------------|
| Line chart | Monthly Incident Trend (`dim_date[month_name]` × Total Incidents) |
| Area chart | High Risk Incidents over time |
| Line chart | Repeat Incident Rate by month |
| Clustered column | `classification` × month (volume trend) |
| Card | Mean Time to Detection (minutes) |

**Slicers:** Date range, `classification`, `stakeholder`

**Narrative:** Trend lines support committee questions (“Are dead-proxy incidents recurring?”) without claiming probabilistic forecasting.

---

## Page 3: Control Testing

**Audience:** IT Audit, Internal Audit, Platform Engineering

| Visual | Fields |
|--------|--------|
| Matrix | `control_name` × `result` (count from `fact_control_tests`) |
| Bar | Control Failure Rate by `control_domain` |
| Clustered bar | PASS vs FAIL vs NOT_TESTED by control |
| Bar | Sum of `limitation_count` by `control_name` |
| Table | Failed controls with `failure_interpretation` (from dimension or tooltip) |

**Slicers:** `control_domain`, `result`, incident date

**Narrative:** NOT_TESTED is expected when incident class is out of control scope. FAIL on CTRL-009 (policy gate) is a release blocker in CI — not an operational KPI miss.

---

## Page 4: Incident Drilldown

**Audience:** IT Support, Cyber Risk triage, Audit sampling

| Visual | Fields |
|--------|--------|
| Table | `fact_incidents` — audit_id, classification, proof_tier, risk_level, status |
| Card | Incidents with Limitations |
| Bar | Count by `dim_proof_tier[proof_tier]` |
| Donut | `policy_action` from `fact_policy_decisions` |
| Drillthrough page | Full incident row + linked control test results |

**Slicers:** `classification`, `proof_tier_key`, `has_limitations`, `human_confirmation_required`

**Drillthrough:** From any incident visual → detail with `limitations[]`, `execution_authority`, audit timestamps.

**Narrative:** T2–T3 proof tiers support triage — they do not authorize destructive remediation without typed confirmation.

---

## Global elements

- **Tooltip page:** Proof tier glossary from `dim_proof_tier`
- **AI transparency card:** AI assists explanation only — does not authorize execution
- **Refresh note:** CSV export is a deterministic snapshot from fixture audit data for portfolio demos
